GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Your next Sydney weekender just got a whole lot more stylish", "Like the rest of us, 2020 has no doubt left you needing to recharge and unwind. And if you're looking for your next escape, Sydney's trifecta of world-class dining, iconic beaches and sunny climate make it the ideal city weekender. And while the NSW capital has long been a popular place to visit, it's a well-worn fact that a trip to the harbour city often ends with an eye-watering hotel bill. Thankfully, you can save your credit card hangover for another day. Little National is Sydney's newest luxury hotel and it's just opened its doors. Located in the thriving CBD hub of Clarence Street, right above Wynyard Station, Little National's convenient location, contemporary fit-out and cleverly designed communal spaces make it an ideal choice for business and leisure travellers alike. Following the success of Little National Canberra, the Sydney outpost is the latest from Australian-based DOMA Group. The hotel group is at the forefront of a new trend in luxury travel that promises luxury experiences without exorbitant hotel charges. It's a low-cost, high value proposition that does away with the usual extraneous add-ons, focusing on the services travellers actually use. An especially refreshing approach, if you're the kind of guest who loves to immerse yourself in a new city, only using the hotel as a place to sleep, eat and charge your mobile phone. Led by an expert interiors team at Bates Smart, the design of the hotel is wholly focused on maximising its guest experience. From a beautifully furnished indoor co-working space to an expansive open-plan rooftop featuring a library working space, garden bar and custom-built fireplace; the public areas of Little National are a sight to behold. Plus, there's even a gym fitted out with the latest Technogym equipment supported with a DOMA Wellness App with curated programs based on the gym equipment. "We wanted to ensure this hotel felt refined and understated, without compromising on quality and space," explains Guy Lake, director at Bates Smart and the mastermind behind the hotel's architecture. "Little National Sydney's design focuses on providing everything you need, but nothing that you don't." Indeed, with its sparkling Sydney skyline views and lush greenery, the rooftop of Little National is a major drawcard, creating a calm oasis for guests to enjoy at their leisure. Inside, rooms have been carefully designed to make use of every inch of space and the natural light that streams in from the feature window wall. Featuring a comfortable bespoke super king-sized bed, with charging ports, SMART television with Chromecast and light controls placed perfectly within arm's reach (no crossing the room to turn out the lights), the layout of Little National's rooms has been carefully considered and executed to the highest level of detail. Rooms are also equipped with a selection of T2 Tea, a virtual mini bar, Appelles Apothecary & Lab bathroom amenities, and there's even free WiFi and movies. With 230 rooms offering five-star service and experiences, without the hefty price tag, Little National offers a slice of affordable luxury for all manner of travellers. Situated within walking distance of Sydney's best shopping and dining precincts, the hotel even has underground rail connections to nearby Barangaroo and George Street. We're sold already."
